title: Detroit Legacy Automakers Outperform EV Pure-Plays in 2024
text: In 2024, Ford and GM stocks quietly outperformed Tesla and Rivian as investors rotated away from EV-focused companies. Legacy automakers demonstrated resilience through established production networks, supply chain advantages, and diverse revenue streams beyond EVs. Ford's F-150 Lightning delivered approximately 12,500 units in the U.S. during 2024, directly competing with Rivian's R1T. GM introduced multiple EV models while leveraging its scale and manufacturing expertise. Legacy automakers brought established infrastructure advantages that pure-EV players like Rivian struggled to match. Brand loyalty, dealer networks, and service infrastructure provided competitive advantages. Cost structures at legacy manufacturers benefited from existing economies of scale. However, Rivian differentiated itself through focus on the adventure and lifestyle segments with its R1T truck and R1S SUV. The R1S SUV captured a 5.1% market share of US EVs over $70,000 by Q1 2024. Rivian's technology focus on autonomous systems and software differentiated it from legacy players. The competitive landscape showed both challenges and opportunities for Rivian as EV market matured.
